_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn simple ways to help us learn, like practising, talking through steps, rewording things and thinking about how we feel about learning.

_source.w: We learn simple ways to help us learn, like practising, talking through steps, rewording things and thinking about how we feel about learning.
y: Learning how to learn is one of the most important skills we can build.
Having strategies for learning makes hard things feel more manageable.
Thinking about our feelings about learning helps us stay positive and motivated.
eg: We practise something several times to get better at it.
We talk through the steps of what we are doing while we do it.
We reword an idea in our own words to help us understand it.
We share how we feel about a task — excited, nervous, confident.
We think about which strategy helps us learn best and use it more.
We celebrate when practising pays off and we improve at something.
